_description: Viral Influencer Trend Reference Group — 10 public creator accounts studied collectively as one benchmark pool for viral travel, lifestyle, ocean, adventure, and storytelling content.
_folder_key: viral_reference_group (physical name kept stable — do not rename folders or config keys)
_purpose: Study viral influencer content, trend formats, and content structures that Avalon Escapes can adapt or recreate. Social media is trend-based — the goal is not to avoid replication entirely. Understand what can be replicated safely and strategically.
_rules: Treat all 10 accounts as ONE collective reference pool, not as individual creators
Raw data is stored per-account for clean re-collection, but analysis always uses the combined group dataset
Data saved to data/raw/viral_reference_group/{username}/ — NEVER mixed with founder or brand data
Always ask for credit confirmation before scraping
Goal: extract the STRUCTURE and TREND FORMATS behind what works, not the content itself

_classification_tiers.1_direct_trend_adaptation: Format is already trend-based — recreate directly with Avalon footage, voice, destinations, captions, and brand angle. (POV formats, send-this-to formats, transition trends, audio trends, list Reels, travel reminders, keyword CTAs, invitation formats)
_classification_tiers.2_structural_replication: Replicate the structure (hook formula, emotional arc, pacing, caption shape, CTA style) but NOT exact words, footage, creator identity, or personal story.
_classification_tiers.3_inspiration_only: Do not copy — too unique to the creator, too personal, or too close to their brand identity. Use only as directional inspiration. (Personal manifestos, signature phrases, private experiences, unique editing identity)

viral_reference_group.label: Viral Influencer Trend Reference Group
viral_reference_group.purpose: Collective benchmark for viral travel / lifestyle / ocean / adventure / storytelling / agency content — trend identification and safe adaptation for Avalon Escapes

collection_settings.note: 25 posts per account for initial pattern analysis. Increase to 50 for deeper historical view.
